threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think the 22 bus is a reliable option. Locals know to skip the bus and just walk; saves time and sanity.
Last night on the 47, a lad was singing to the driver—got a chorus going with the regulars. Made the late ride feel more like a pub evening.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a local kid selling homemade lemonade next to a graffiti mural, and the old man buying a cup gave him a thumbs up. That's Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You can grab a döner kebab at 3 AM while a street artist plays violin in the middle of the Kreuzberg night. That's Berlin for you.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot the locals by how they nonchalantly avoid the BART delays while newcomers stand confused, clutching their phones.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Just saw a guy in a tuxedo hop on the 31 at Turk, he looked like he just came from a wedding. Only in SF do you see that kind of vibe on the Muni.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about Gaudí, but the real gems are the tiny plazas where locals sip café con leche, ignoring the hype.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They still take photos of the pavement mosaics in Gràcia, thinking it’s some hidden gem. Locals just walk over them, used to the beauty.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still ask for directions at the Dalston Junction station instead of just getting the 242 like everyone else does.
Caught a guy serenading the 77 bus with a ukulele last night. Never thought I’d hear ‘Wonderwall’ at 2 AM. The driver just smiled.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a spontaneous dance party break out at the Hermannplatz U-Bahn station, I knew Neukölln had me hooked.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: In Kreuzberg, it's normal to find a café where you can sip coffee while a dog munches on vegan cake. Only in Berlin do pets enjoy brunch too.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by how they navigate an overcrowded BART with that 'I know this isn't my train' calm, while newcomers just panic at each delay.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Just saw a guy on the 31 bus randomly break into opera mid-ride. The tourists were confused, but the locals just smiled and nodded like it was another Tuesday.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all the bars have the same vibe, but locals know the best ones are hidden behind unmarked doors. That’s where the real fun is.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell a newbie by the way they still attempt to order a 'café con leche' in the afternoon. Locals know it’s for breakfast only.
